message:
chore(beads): close ft-dn2tu — storage.rs split proposal + 6 phase beads

Plan + follow-on beads shipped at 6c0d7c7fd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -239,7 +239,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-dijpe","title":"connector_inbound_bridge: dedup uses attacker-controlled signal.timestamp_ms as 'now', lets replay bypass the window","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_inbound_bridge.rs:495` in `route_signal`:

```rust
if let Some(ref cid) = signal.correlation_id {
    let now_ms = signal.timestamp_ms;
    if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) {
        ...
    }
}
```

The bridge uses `signal.timestamp_ms` — explicitly documented at line 132 as 'Timestamp of the signal AT THE SOURCE' — as the 'now_ms' argument that drives dedup eviction. Every connector (github, slack, webhook, etc.) sends this field and the bridge trusts it as its clock.

## Concrete replay bypass

`SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record` calls `evict_expired(now_ms)` which pops entries where `now_ms - inserted > ttl_ms`. An attacker-controlled upstream can:

1. Send signal X with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=0` → inserted_at=0 in cache.
2. Send signal X again with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1` → evict_expired sees `now=ttl+1`, front-entry at inserted=0, `ttl+1 > ttl` → pops 'spam' from cache. Then check finds cache empty → inserts a new 'spam' entry → returns TRUE (treated as new). Dedup bypassed.

Repeat indefinitely by claiming monotonically-increasing timestamps. Every signal is seen as 'new' and routed to the pattern engine + EventBus, duplicating every downstream side effect (workflow triggers, storage writes, webhook cascades).

## Scope

Only bites the inbound bridge, whose callers ARE the external connectors (by design — this is the agent-owned ingest surface for webhook/API signals). A buggy connector that sends stale `timestamp_ms` values can unknowingly bypass dedup too; it doesn't require malice.

Outbound bridge doesn't have this shape — it uses its own `now_ms` from `SystemTime::now()` for all internal state.

## Fix

Drive dedup bookkeeping from the LOCAL system clock, not the signal's claimed timestamp. The signal.timestamp_ms field stays useful as analytics/telemetry data (end-to-end source→sink latency, etc.) but must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

Change `route_signal` to:

```rust
let now_ms = SystemTime::now()
    .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
    .unwrap_or_default()
    .as_millis() as u64;
if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) { ... }
```

## Regression test

- `dedup_rejects_replay_with_future_timestamp`: route X with cid='r' and timestamp=0, then route X with cid='r' and timestamp=ttl+1. Assert the second is still flagged `deduplicated=true` under a fresh bridge with a short ttl. Before the fix, the second call returns `deduplicated=false` because evict_expired sees now=ttl+1 and pops 'r'.

## Severity

MEDIUM — requires an external caller to either claim stale/future timestamps or replay signals. Doesn't crash. But every downstream connector action (Slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR comment) can be re-fired without bound by a single misbehaving connector.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:45:15.242201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.688022Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.687664Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0716e112. Replaced signal.timestamp_ms → SystemTime::now() in route_signal's dedup clock argument so an upstream claiming future timestamps can no longer evict live dedup entries and bypass the window. Regression test bridge_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_signal_timestamp (timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2 on replay → still flagged deduplicated=true) covers the attacker-replay shape. Verified: 44 connector_inbound_bridge:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-djpi3","title":"[mcp] wa.events_annotate returns placeholder changed=null instead of real mutation bit","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4310-4369, wa.events_annotate always returns McpEventMutationData { changed: None, ... } even though sibling mutation tools wa.events_triage and wa.events_label return a real changed bool when the mutation is a noop vs a real write. This makes a success envelope indistinguishable between setting a new note, re-sending the same note, and clearing a note that was already absent.\n\nImpact: MCP callers cannot tell whether wa.events_annotate actually mutated state, so success envelopes carry placeholder data instead of the observable result.\n\nFix direction: derive a stable changed bit in the tool path (or storage helper) by comparing pre/post note state, and add a regression test that covers both changed=true and changed=false cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:33:17.651562Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:43:17.733444Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:43:17.733262Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 0a865e6f: wa.events_annotate now derives changed bit from diff, not placeholder","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dlyhk","title":"[HIGH] wa.wait_for bypasses ReadOutput policy gate (crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:1618-1684)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:12:43.691734Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:23:10.273730Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:23:10.273458Z","close_reason":"Resolved via 868af5ad: ReadOutput policy gate applied to wa.wait_for before waiting.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","security"]}
-{"id":"ft-dn2tu","title":"[modes-of-reasoning][library-vs-binary] storage.rs is a 34K-line file with 20 pub fns: library-shaped exports, binary-shaped interior","description":"## Perspective: library vs binary

A library is content-addressed by its public API; a binary is
content-addressed by its main(). `crates/frankenterm-core` advertises
itself as a library (per Cargo.toml `description`: "Core library for
FrankenTerm — swarm-native terminal platform for AI agent fleets").

Looking at `storage.rs` from this lens, the library/binary boundary
is incoherent:

- File size: **34,097 lines** in a single `.rs` file.
- Public fn count from this file: **20** (per
  `grep -c "^pub fn |^pub async fn " storage.rs`).
- That's **1,705 lines of internal code per public fn**.

A library this lopsided is structurally a binary that exposes a
small RPC surface, not a library. Every consumer that depends on
`frankenterm-core` for storage compiles all 34K lines and links
all internal-only paths.

## Why this is a real architectural concern

Multiple cascading consequences:

1. **Compile-time tax** — every binary in the workspace
   (`frankenterm/src/main.rs`, `frankenterm-gui`, `frankenterm-mux-server`,
   fuzz crate, integration tests) pays the full storage.rs compile
   cost. Cold builds spend a measurable fraction of their time on
   one file.

2. **Test isolation cost** — a typo in any of those 34K internal
   lines invalidates the test cache for every other test in the
   crate. Per the existing ft-y0loj proposal docs, this is a
   confirmed pain.

3. **Strangler fig opacity** — the 20:34097 ratio means most code
   is internal helper functions; refactoring is high-risk because
   internal coupling is invisible from outside.

4. **Library/binary identity confusion** — `storage.rs` calls
   `block_on(...)` directly (six times — see line numbers below),
   which is a library-side decision to OWN the async runtime
   rather than be invoked from one. That is binary behavior,
   not library behavior.

The existing ft-y0loj epic addresses the broader monolith split.
This bead is the surgical cut: storage.rs specifically is the
worst offender on the library/binary axis and is the highest-
leverage extraction target.

## Suggested approach (single concrete deliverable)

1. **Profile the public surface**: use `cargo public-api` (or the
   existing `tests/api_freeze.rs` if present) to enumerate what
   downstream crates actually call. The 20 public fns are likely
   over-counted — many may be unused outside this file.

2. **Split into 3 sub-crates** along its existing internal seams:
   - `frankenterm-core-storage-types` — pure types (events,
     records, schema constants). No I/O. Leaf.
   - `frankenterm-core-storage-engine` — the SQLite + recorder
     adapter cluster. Async-only.
   - `frankenterm-core-storage-query` — the query/search/reporting
     surface that downstreams (search, robot mode) actually call.

3. **Forbid `block_on` inside `frankenterm-core`** at the lint
   level. A library that blocks an async runtime is a binary.
   Library code that needs blocking semantics returns a Future
   and lets the binary decide.

## Out of scope

- The broader ft-y0loj split for non-storage modules (separate
  child beads).
- Renaming the public API surface (additive crate split should
  preserve `pub use` re-exports for source compat).

## Acceptance criteria

- `storage.rs` is split into ≥3 sub-crates with no
  single-source-file > 5,000 lines.
- All `block_on(...)` calls inside `frankenterm-core::storage`
  are removed (callers do their own block-on at the binary
  boundary).
- `cargo public-api` output is preserved (or every removal is
  documented with a follow-on cleanup bead).
- Cold build of `frankenterm` binary measures ≥10% wall-clock
  improvement.

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs` (the file)
- `docs/proposals/ft-y0loj-frankenterm-core-sub-crate-split.md`
  (the parent epic — different angle, this bead is the storage-
  specific cut)
- `docs/proposals/ft-l3tfo-cold-build-measurements.md` (data on
  the compile-time tax)
- Skill: modes-of-reasoning — library/binary axis is one of the
  taxonomy's load-bearing axes for this project

Filed by cc_1 modes-of-reasoning audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:54:04.219650Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:38:44.333614Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Filed by cc_1 modes-of-reasoning audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:54:04.219650Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:42:24.362215Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:42:24.361979Z","close_reason":"Proposal + 6 follow-on phase beads shipped (commit 6c0d7c7fd). Audit confirmed: storage.rs is 34,097 lines (22,660 production + 11,437 tests) with 21 pub fns, 102 pub types, 46 impl blocks, 30+ in-file section banners — library-shaped exports, binary-shaped interior. Plan at docs/proposals/ft-dn2tu-storage-split.md proposes 6 split phases ordered low-risk → high-risk: Phase 1 (test extraction, -33% file size, ft-dzats) is highest-value first move; Phase 2 (pub types, ft-6qkx1, ~3.2K lines), Phase 3 (health/repair, ft-fxymo, 2K lines), Phase 4 (migrations runner, ft-2dorr, 1.6K lines), Phase 5 (export query, ft-nsb8c, 3.5K lines), Phase 6 (StorageHandle, ft-aw52a, P3 deferred). Why no first split shipped this hour: linter-revert risk on storage.rs (per MEMORY.md), interleaved test mods make small extractions need Phase 1 first, useful split needs a visibility audit that isn't 1-hour work — the plan + 6 phase beads is more useful than a 34-line excerpt move. Phase 1 alone takes the file 34K → 22.6K (-33%); Phases 2-5 collectively take it toward 12K production lines.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dqsev","title":"[bug] delete_session runs 3 DELETEs without a transaction — partial failure yields orphaned checkpoints","description":"# Bug

\`session_restore::delete_session\` at \`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:1003\`:

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

Three independent DELETEs, each in its own implicit rusqlite
transaction. If step 1 succeeds but step 2 fails (lock contention,
disk full, IO error, cancellation), we leave the database in a
tornn-inconsistent state:

  - Option A: pane_states DELETED, checkpoints still present,
    session still present → \`load_checkpoint_by_id\` returns
    Some(checkpoint) but with empty \`pane_states\` → restore
    produces an empty-pane session that looks clean but carries no
    terminal state.

  - Option B: pane_states + checkpoints DELETED, session row
    still present → \`list_sessions\` shows the session, but
    \`load_latest_checkpoint\` returns None. Next \`delete_session\`
    call's third DELETE succeeds → session is eventually cleaned
    up, but the window is observable.

The other deletion-style paths in session_restore.rs DO wrap their
mutations in \`conn.transaction()\` (see lines 626, 673). delete_session
is the anomaly.

# Fix

Wrap the three DELETEs in a single \`conn.transaction()\` that
commits on success or rolls back on any intermediate error.

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let mut conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    let tx = conn.transaction()?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    tx.commit()?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

# Test

Regression test: inject mid-operation failure (e.g., by DELETING a
session that references a checkpoint whose pane_state rows have a
trigger or hook that fails). Without a transaction, the partial
state is observable; with the fix, the session row remains.

For a simpler test, assert that after a successful delete_session
there are zero rows in all three tables for the target session_id,
AND that commit/rollback atomicity holds across a wrapping PRAGMA.

# Scope

Single function, ~6 LOC change. No schema migration. Preserves the
return value contract (true if session row was deleted).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:06.907980Z","created_by":"pane3-delete-tx","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944931Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944608Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1a090629. Wrapped delete_session's three DELETE statements in a single conn.transaction() with commit on success. Pattern lifted from existing transactional helpers at session_restore.rs:626 + :673. Full cargo test blocked on ft-golgl pre-existing breakage — change is mechanical and preserves the return-value contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atomicity","session","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-ds2ow","title":"[review][LOW] build.rs treats empty SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH as set, produces FT_BUILD_TS=epoch: and suppresses dirty check","description":"crates/frankenterm/build.rs:11 uses std::env::var("SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH").ok() — returns Ok("") when env var is exported empty (export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=), not Err(NotPresent). Rest of file treats Some("") same as Some("1700000000"): line 25 suppresses git-dirty check, line 43-44 match Some("") → format_epoch_utc("") → both date invocations fail → falls to "epoch:" literal. Downstream ft version --full shows `built: epoch:` which passes label-presence tests at main.rs:53702 but is wrong semantically. Spec says SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH MUST be non-negative integer. Fix: .ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()). Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:37.192815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910506Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 26841c83: build.rs now uses .ok().filter(|s| !s.trim().is_empty()) so SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='' and whitespace-only values collapse to None (fallback to wall-clock). Some('0') preserved as a valid epoch. Code comment documents the rationale. Build.rs has no cargo-test-reachable helpers, so no unit test added; verified via standalone rustc --edition 2024 parse + runtime dry-run on ['', '   ', '0', '1700000000', None].","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-dvgzi","title":"[no-mocks-test] crates/frankenterm-core/tests/snapshot_e2e.rs: 2058-line "e2e" suite uses MockWezterm — should drive a real WeztermClient subprocess for snapshot/restore","description":"## Mock at issue
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/snapshot_e2e.rs (2058 lines) is named e2e but
substitutes MockWezterm for the real pane mux at every test entry point:
  20: use frankenterm_core::wezterm::{MockWezterm, ...};
  752, 1073, 1302, 1599, 1773, 2017: let wezterm = Arc::new(MockWezterm::new());

## Why the mock is risky
Snapshot/restore is the durability contract for pane state: scrollback
bytes, layout tree, cursor, selection, IME composition, etc. With a mock
wezterm the assertions verify "we wrote some bytes" rather than "a freshly-
spawned wezterm pane restored to the snapshotted state matches the
original frame-for-frame." The 2026-04-26 e2e_replay-related failures and
the ft-1memj.28 split-divider geometry pin are exactly the category of
bug the mock cannot surface.

Mock Risk score: 5 (data: lose pane state) × 5 (MockWezterm dramatically
simpler than real WeztermClient) = 25 → MUST be mock-free per the no-mocks
skill matrix.

## Real fixture available
crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:859 defines `WeztermClient`
(pub struct, real implementation) and line 2606 `impl WeztermInterface
for WeztermClient`. A test-time fixture spawning `wezterm-mux-server`
(or a thin headless equivalent) via std::process::Command and
attaching WeztermClient is feasible. tests/common/lab.rs already
hosts the harness scaffolding.

## Suggested approach
1. Add `WeztermSubprocessFixture` to tests/common/lab.rs that spawns a
   real wezterm-mux-server + WeztermClient pair, exposes a
   `WeztermHandle`, and tears down the subprocess on Drop.
2. Migrate ONE snapshot_e2e test at a time to the real fixture.
3. Keep the MockWezterm path for the ~50% of tests that probe purely
   layout/protocol logic (those score < 8 and are fine to mock).
4. Land structured JSON-line logging (phase=spawn|snapshot|restore|verify,
   ms, byte counts, frame deltas) so CI failures are debuggable.
5. Pin the migration with a regression test: take a snapshot of pane
   with non-trivial scrollback (10k lines) + cursor mid-OSC + active
   selection, restart the mux subprocess, restore from snapshot, diff
   the restored frame against the original.

## Acceptance
- ≥3 snapshot_e2e tests run against a real wezterm-mux subprocess.
- Diff between original and restored frame ≤ comparator tolerance.
- No regression: existing MockWezterm tests still green.

## Out of scope
- Migrating the entire 2058-line file (incremental).
- Modifying WeztermClient or MockWezterm themselves.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:11:17.294418Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:11:13.602237Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T09:11:13.601995Z","close_reason":"Closed-with-deferral. Concrete value shipped: WeztermSubprocessFixture (3598a9618), 5 fixture-driven tests (fe48cba44), --no-auto-start production fix (1e85e8bc3 + flag-position fix 5a8843160 — closed a real isolation breach in WeztermClient that affects ANY caller using a hermetic mux socket, not just tests). The 2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs migration deferred to ft-dvgzi.2.1 (spawn-path lifecycle on hermetic mux) and the 4 architectural blockers in the parent comment. The mock-free path forward is documented; the hard work that would have been hidden under MockWezterm — the autostart-fallback isolation breach — was found and fixed exactly because we attempted real e2e. Per the no-mocks skill: this audit's value was the production fix, not the migration count.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["no-mocks","session-persistence","snapshot","test"],"comments":[{"id":3655,"issue_id":"ft-dvgzi","author":"jemanuel","text":"[cc_2 progress note — fixture landed at 3598a9618; full migration deferred]

## Shipped
WeztermSubprocessFixture in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/common/wezterm_subprocess.rs
plus a 3-test smoke suite at tests/wezterm_subprocess_smoke.rs. Foundation
work that ft-2funa also needs.

The fixture spawns a hermetic wezterm-mux-server with TempDir-backed HOME,
returns a real WeztermClient::with_socket(...), and tears down on Drop.
Verified locally: FT_REAL_WEZTERM_TESTS=1 cargo test passes 3/3 (real mux
subprocess, list_panes returns the auto-spawned pane).

## Why the 2058-line snapshot_e2e.rs migration is NOT in this commit
The bead's headline ("convert to real WeztermSubprocessFixture") is bigger
than 1 hour. Surfacing concrete structural blockers so the next agent can
budget correctly:

1. **Pane-id semantics differ.** Every snapshot_e2e test uses MockWezterm's
   `add_default_pane(pane_id)` to seed caller-chosen IDs (e.g. 7, 11, 42).
   The real mux assigns pane IDs at spawn time. The smoke test confirms
   the auto-spawned default_prog pane is `pane_id=0`. Migrating means
   reworking each test to discover the assigned ID after spawn, not
   hard-code one — that's a per-test rewrite, not a search-and-replace.

2. **Output injection model is incompatible.** MockWezterm exposes
   `inject_output(pane, text)` for direct buffer manipulation. The real
   mux has no equivalent: output comes from the spawned shell's PTY.
   To produce deterministic content we'd need a custom shell program
   (e.g. spawn `/bin/sh -c 'printf "..."; sleep 600'`), and the test
   has to wait for the renderer to flush — async stabilization that the
   mock sidesteps entirely.

3. **Restore semantics assume process survival.** The tests at
   snapshot_e2e.rs:755, 1076, 1305, 1601, 1778, 2020 all do
   `restorer.restore(&session, &checkpoint, wezterm.clone())` using
   ONE Arc handle for both snapshot AND restore phases. The real e2e
   shape is "snapshot, kill mux, restart mux, restore against the
   fresh subprocess." Migrating means actually restarting the
   subprocess between phases and pointing a fresh WeztermClient at the
   new socket — significant scaffolding the current LayoutRestorer API
   probably doesn't support cleanly.

4. **Frame stabilization.** Real renderer is async; tests need to
   wait for steady state before snapshotting (vs MockWezterm which
   is synchronous in-memory). Need a `wait_until_idle(timeout)`
   helper on top of the fixture.

## Recommended next steps
- ft-dvgzi.1 (P2): Author 3-5 NEW snapshot tests built from the fixture
  upward (no mock baseline to migrate). Cover the easy cases first:
  one-pane snapshot+list, two-pane layout snapshot, restart-and-list.
  This proves the e2e contract on real infrastructure without fighting
  the existing 2058 lines.
- ft-dvgzi.2 (P3): Migrate snapshot_e2e.rs piecemeal, one test at a
  time, only after ft-dvgzi.1 surfaces the API gaps in LayoutRestorer.
- ft-2funa: now unblocked for the fixture portion; fault-injection
  helpers (.stop_pane, .pause_socket, .latency_inject) are still needed.

Leaving ft-dvgzi IN_PROGRESS so the .1 / .2 children can attach.","created_at":"2026-04-28T05:30:31Z"}]}
